mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so 20-day S&P 500 returns turn positive after clear cuts in policy rates. The signal is invalid if rate cuts coincide with rising inflation or if the market has already fully priced the easing.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS has fallen over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when payroll growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign weakens when UNRATE rises without concurrent PAYEMS slowdown.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when sales growth is driven only by inflation rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Rejected if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not outperform baseline in 20-day SPX returns, especially when CPIAUCSL acceleration explains the sales gain more than real spending.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling inflation signals improving real output without overheating, which should lift earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The mechanism breaks when industrial production rises alongside a forcing inflation surge and tighter rates.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day SPX returns are not higher when INDPRO trends up while CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS do not accelerate, or if the relation reverses in repeated rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST is a leading indicator of residential demand and credit-sensitive activity, so it should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when consumer inflation is contained and policy rates are not rising.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if months where HOUST crosses above its 6-month average do not show positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are stable.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate above prior-cycle normal levels tightens discount rates and suppresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be most visible when inflation is not falling quickly enough to justify easing.
failure_condition: Rejected if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is falling faster than rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS with a stable or declining UNRATE indicates job creation is broad enough to support income growth and equity demand, which should lead to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The signal fails when payroll gains are offset by a simultaneous rise in unemployment.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day SPX returns are not higher after sustained payroll gains, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is flat-to-down.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth together with weak RSAFS signals broad final-demand deceleration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is invalid if GDP slows only because of temporary inventory effects while sales remain firm.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with slowing GDPC1 and RSAFS do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship flips positive in repeated out-of-sample windows.
